title: Anticancer Drugs (Part 2): Alkylating Agents, Microtubule Inhibitors, Hormones & Others
summary: العوامل الالكيلية CCNS تعمل بنقل مجموعات الكيل الى DNA مما يمنع تضاعفه وتشمل الميكلورثامين والسيكلوفوسفاميد والنيتروزيورياز. مثبطات الانابيب الدقيقة CCS تمنع انقسام الخلية بالتاثير على المغزل الميتوزي وتشمل الفينكا الكالكالويد والباكليتاكسيل. الهرمونات ومضاداتها تستخدم للاورام الحساسة للهرمونات كالتاموكسيفين في سرطان الثدي. السيسبلاتين والكاربوبلاتين والانترفيرون تكمل الترسانة العلاجية.

key_drugs.name: Mechlorethamine
key_drugs.note: تسريب من الوريد، اذا خرج خارج الوريد: Sodium Thiosulfate

key_drugs.name: Cyclophosphamide
key_drugs.note: Hemorrhagic Cystitis بسبب Acrolein - يُمنع بـ Mesna وكثرة السوائل

key_drugs.name: Lomustine / Carmustine
key_drugs.note: يعبران حاجز الدم-الدماغ - لعلاج اورام الدماغ

key_drugs.name: Paclitaxel
key_drugs.note: يستلزم تحضيرا مسبقا بـ Dexamethasone + Diphenhydramine + H2 blocker

key_drugs.name: Doxorubicin
key_drugs.moa: تداخل DNA + تثبيط topoisomerase II + جذور حرة

mnemonics.title: السيكلوفوسفاميد والمثانة
mnemonics.body: تخيّل Acrolein كحمض يسكب على جدار المثانة فيحرقها (Hemorrhagic Cystitis). Mesna = اسفنجة تمص الحمض قبل ان يلمس الجدار. كثرة الماء = تخفيف التركيز.

mnemonics.title: BBB = اللواتي يعبرن الحاجز الدماغي
mnemonics.body:
B = BBB (Blood Brain Barrier)
B = loMustine و carMustine (Nitrosoureas)
B = Brain tumors = علاجهن!
القاعدة: النيتروزيورياز = الدماغ

mnemonics.title: VX vs VBL: عصب مقابل نخاع
mnemonics.body: VinCristine = يكره الCNS = يسبب نيوروباثي (N = Neuro = Cristine). VinBlastine = يضرب Blast خلايا الدم = تثبيط النخاع اشد. كلاهما يعبر عن هويته في اسمه.

mnemonics.title: السيسبلاتين والكلية
mnemonics.body: تخيّل حبة Cisplatin كحجر بلاتيني ثقيل يسقط على خلايا الانابيب الكلوية القريبة (Proximal Tubule) ويسحقها. الماء الغزير = رافعة تحمل الحجر بعيدا عن الكلية.

mnemonics.title: TAXI = Paclitaxel يحتاج تحضيرا
mnemonics.body:
T = Taxane (Paclitaxel)
A = Antihistamine (Diphenhydramine)
X = (De)Xamethasone
I = H2 Inhibitor
قبل الباكليتاكسيل: اركب الTAXI!

mnemonics.title: Chemo Man — سُميّات العلاج الكيميائي
mnemonics.body: خريطة الجسم: الرأس/الأعصاب N = Nitrosoureas (عصبي، يعبر BBB). العيون/الأذن C = Cisplatin/Carboplatin (نيفرو/أوتو). القلب D = Doxorubicin/Daunorubicin (سُمّية قلبية). الصدر B = Bleomycin/Busulfan (تليف رئوي). البطن MF = Methotrexate + 5-FU (كبت نقي العظم). المثانة P = cycloPhosphamide (التهاب مثانة نزفي → اعطِ Mesna). الأطراف V = Vinca alkaloids (اعتلال أعصاب طرفي). Tamoxifen → تغيّر حدّة الإبصار.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of alkylating agents?
flashcards.back: Transfer of alkyl groups to the N7 position of guanine bases in DNA, causing strand breaks, cross-linking between strands, and prevention of normal replication.

flashcards.front: What is the treatment for Mechlorethamine extravasation?
flashcards.back: Sodium Thiosulfate is injected locally to neutralize the extravasated drug.

flashcards.front: What is Acrolein and what is its relationship to Cyclophosphamide?
flashcards.back: Acrolein is a toxic metabolite of Cyclophosphamide that causes Hemorrhagic Cystitis (bladder inflammation). Prevented by Mesna and increased fluid intake.

flashcards.front: What is the use of Streptozotocin and its distinctive toxicity?
flashcards.back: Used in Insulinoma because it selectively destroys beta cells of the islets of Langerhans. Distinctive toxicity: causes diabetes mellitus (Diabetogenic).

flashcards.front: What feature of Nitrosoureas makes them suitable for brain tumors?
flashcards.back: Lomustine and Carmustine cross the blood-brain barrier (BBB), unlike most anticancer drugs.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Vinca Alkaloids?
flashcards.back: They bind to Tubulin and inhibit its polymerization, causing dissolution of the mitotic spindle and arrest of cell division in the M phase.

flashcards.front: What is the difference in toxicity between Vincristine and Vinblastine?
flashcards.back: Vincristine (VX): peripheral neuropathy, loss of reflexes, muscle weakness. Vinblastine (VBL): more severe bone marrow suppression than VX.

flashcards.front: What is the clinical use of Vincristine?
flashcards.back: In the POMP protocol for leukemia, Wilms tumor, and other cancers — always used in combination with other drugs.

flashcards.front: Why is a premedication protocol given before Paclitaxel?
flashcards.back: Due to the risk of severe hypersensitivity reaction. Protocol: Dexamethasone + Diphenhydramine + H2 blocker.

flashcards.front: What is Tamoxifen and what is its use?
flashcards.back: SERM (Selective Estrogen Receptor Modulator) — an antiestrogen with weak estrogenic activity. First-line treatment for ER-positive breast cancer.

flashcards.front: What is the problem with estrogens in the treatment of prostate cancer?
flashcards.back: They cause myocardial infarction, thromboembolic events, stroke, and sexual dysfunction.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a hormone-responsive and a hormone-dependent tumor?
flashcards.back: Responsive: regresses when the hormone is given (palliative only). Dependent: regresses when the hormone is removed by surgery or drugs.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Cisplatin renal toxicity and how is it prevented?
flashcards.back: It damages proximal tubule cells. Prevented by aggressive hydration and forced diuresis.

flashcards.front: What is the dangerous drug interaction with Cisplatin?
flashcards.back: Aminoglycosides increase both nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity when combined with Cisplatin.

flashcards.front: What are the specific side effects of Interferon-alpha?
flashcards.back: Depression is the most important. Also: flu-like symptoms (fever, chills) in the first days, leukopenia and thrombocytopenia (dose-limiting), and elevated liver enzymes.

flashcards.front: What is G-CSF and what is its use in chemotherapy?
flashcards.back: Filgrastim (Granulocyte Colony-Stimulating Factor) is given with Paclitaxel to prevent severe Neutropenia.

flashcards.front: What is the risk of long-term use of alkylating agents?
flashcards.back: They are mutagenic and can lead to secondary malignancies, especially acute leukemia, 10 or more years later.

flashcards.front: Why is Prednisone used in cancer treatment?
flashcards.back: To reduce inflammation, prevent hypersensitivity reactions to chemotherapy, suppress immunity after bone marrow transplant, and directly against lymphomas and leukemias.

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive toxicity of Doxorubicin/Daunorubicin?
flashcards.back: Cumulative cardiotoxicity (monitor with Dexrazoxane).

flashcards.front: What is the distinctive toxicity of Bleomycin?
flashcards.back: Pulmonary fibrosis (get baseline CXR); minimal bone marrow suppression.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Vinca alkaloids?
flashcards.back: Inhibit polymerization of microtubules → arrest cell division in M phase.

flashcards.front: What is the difference in toxicity between Vincristine and Vinblastine?
flashcards.back: Vincristine → peripheral neuropathy; Vinblastine → bone marrow suppression.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Taxanes (Paclitaxel)?
flashcards.back: Stabilize microtubules and prevent their depolymerization → arrest cell division; causes neuropathy and neutropenia.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Mechlorethamine?
flashcards.back: Nitrogen mustard alkylating agent that transfers an alkyl group to N7-guanine; causes severe nausea and bone marrow suppression.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism and toxicity of Tamoxifen?
flashcards.back: Antiestrogen for breast cancer; risk of endometrial cancer and visual disturbances.

flashcards.front: How is severe chemotherapy-induced nausea managed?
flashcards.back: Ondansetron (5-HT3 antagonist) + Dexamethasone before treatment.

mcqs.q: A patient taking Cyclophosphamide presents with hematuria. What is the cause and what is the preventive treatment?
options: Acrolein causes Hemorrhagic Cystitis — Mesna and increased fluids
mcqs.explanation: Acrolein, the toxic metabolite of Cyclophosphamide, burns the bladder wall. Mesna binds and neutralizes it before it contacts the bladder.

mcqs.q: Which of the following drugs crosses the blood-brain barrier and is used for brain tumors?
mcqs.explanation: Carmustine and Lomustine (Nitrosoureas) have the ability to cross the BBB, making them suitable for brain tumors.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism by which Vincristine and Vinblastine work?
options: Prevention of Tubulin polymerization and dissolution of the mitotic spindle
mcqs.explanation: Vinca Alkaloids bind to Tubulin and inhibit its polymerization, causing dissolution of the mitotic spindle and arrest of cell division.

mcqs.q: Vincristine causes systemic neurological side effects including:
options: Loss of reflexes (Areflexia) and muscle weakness
mcqs.explanation: Vincristine causes characteristic peripheral neuropathy: loss of reflexes and muscle weakness (especially Quadriceps). Constipation results from autonomic neuropathy.

mcqs.q: A female patient with ER-positive breast cancer — what is the appropriate first-line drug?
mcqs.explanation: Tamoxifen is a SERM that blocks estrogen receptors in breast cells and is the first-line treatment for ER-positive breast cancer.

mcqs.q: Before infusing Paclitaxel, a premedication regimen must be given including:
mcqs.explanation: Paclitaxel causes severe hypersensitivity reactions requiring premedication with Dexamethasone (corticosteroid) + Diphenhydramine (antihistamine) + H2 blocker.

mcqs.q: Which drug reduces Paclitaxel-induced bone marrow toxicity (Neutropenia)?
mcqs.explanation: Filgrastim (G-CSF) stimulates white blood cell production and is given prophylactically against Paclitaxel-induced Neutropenia.

mcqs.q: The distinctive side effect of Cisplatin that differs from other alkylating agents is:
mcqs.explanation: Cisplatin is distinctive for its nephrotoxicity (Proximal Tubule) and ototoxicity. Both are increased when combined with Aminoglycosides.

options: Insulinoma because it selectively destroys beta cells
mcqs.explanation: Streptozotocin is a nitrosourea with selectivity for pancreatic beta cells, used in Insulinoma (insulin-secreting tumor).

mcqs.q: The specific side effects of Interferon-alpha include:
options: Depression + flu-like symptoms (fever, chills) in the first days
mcqs.explanation: IFN-alpha is distinctive for depression (especially with Alpha) and flu-like symptoms in the first days of treatment.

high_yield.point: Doxorubicin/Daunorubicin → سُمّية قلبية تراكمية (راقب بـ Dexrazoxane).
Bleomycin → تليف رئوي (اعمل CXR أساسي)؛ أقل كبت نقوي.
Vincristine → اعتلال أعصاب طرفي؛ Vinblastine → كبت نقوي.
Tamoxifen → تغيّر حدّة الإبصار/سُمّية قرنية؛ مضاد إستروجين.
